does: This paper introduces CUFG, a machine unlearning framework that erases specific knowledge from trained models while maintaining their stability and reliability. The core contribution combines two innovations: a gradient corrector mechanism guided by forgetting gradients that stabilizes fine-tuning-based unlearning, and a curriculum learning approach that progressively forgets data samples ordered from easy to hard. Rather than aggressive interventions like gradient ascent or random label corruption, CUFG uses gentler, more controlled forgetting mechanisms. The method is evaluated against existing unlearning approaches and the gold-standard complete retraining baseline, demonstrating improved stability, effectiveness, and proximity to retrained models while reducing computational cost.
connects: This work extends recent research in machine unlearning, which has emerged as a critical capability for privacy compliance and security in deep learning. It responds to documented limitations of prior approximate unlearning methods—gradient ascent-based approaches, influence function techniques, and noise-injection strategies—that achieve forgetting at the cost of model instability and weight collapse. The paper builds on curriculum learning principles, well-established in supervised learning for improving generalization, by adapting them to the unlearning setting. It also relates to broader work on fine-tuning-based model editing and controlled model modification, combining insights from gradient-based optimization, continual learning stability literature, and privacy-preserving machine learning.
enables: This research opens avenues for developing more reliable unlearning systems suitable for real-world deployment where model reliability cannot be sacrificed for data removal. The curriculum unlearning paradigm could inspire future work on optimal forgetting schedules and adaptive sample difficulty assessment. The gradient corrector mechanism provides a foundation for designing other stability-aware unlearning operators beyond fine-tuning. Practically, CUFG enables organizations to satisfy data privacy regulations and respond to user deletion requests without expensive model retraining or deploying unstable models. Future work could explore combining CUFG with other modalities, scaling to larger models, and characterizing theoretical convergence properties and unlearning guarantees.
